addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Strengthen ZopfliLZ77Optimal specification with mutation testing

1	---
2	name: zlz77opt-depth200-vacuity
3	description: ZopfliLZ77Optimal verifies @200 with sound Fixed-sibling spec but 0/28 kills (depth-200 vacuity)
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: cf4817c1-02eb-4ae1-a917-45d46c8332fa
8	---
9	
10	ZopfliLZ77Optimal (zopfli.c ~5247) — the squeeze driver, sibling of
11	[[zlof-depth200-vacuity]] (ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed). Verifies under the official
12	runner with a sound spec mirroring the Fixed sibling:
13	requires is_fresh(s)+is_fresh(store)+instart<=inend+ (inend-instart <
14	max_malloc_size/sizeof(float)-1); assigns(object_whole(s), object_whole(store)).
15	Unlike Fixed it never sets s->blockstart/blockend itself (GetBestLengths/
16	FollowPath don't either), so NO exact blockstart/blockend ensures available.
17	
18	0/28 kills — inherent depth-200 vacuity, do NOT chase. Official flow is dfcc
19	enforce-contract with __CPROVER__start harness, all callee bodies INLINED (not
20	replaced), cbmc --depth 200. The whole call tree (greedy, LZ77OptimalRun→
21	GetBestLengths, ZopfliCalculateBlockSize→EncodeTree→BoundaryPM recursion) inlines
22	deep; every mutant's effect is past the depth-200 frontier:
23	- malloc-size mutants (blocksize-1, inend+instart): OOB only manifests deep
24	  inside inlined LZ77OptimalRun array writes; nothing caller-visible (arrays are
25	  local + freed). The zlof OBJECT_SIZE-callee-requires kill trick does NOT apply
26	  here because official flow INLINES (doesn't replace) LZ77OptimalRun.
27	- 7 verbose mutants (line `if (verbose_more || (verbose && cost<bestcost))`):
28	  EQUIVALENT — only affect an fprintf to stderr, no observable contract effect.
29	- loop-cond / cost<bestcost / i>5&&cost==lastcost / lastrandomstep mutants: all
30	  control which store/stats get produced, observable only after executing the
31	  huge inlined loop body, past depth 200.
32	
33	Tooling notes: plain `goto-instrument --enforce-contract` (legacy, non-dfcc)
34	CRASHES here — "Recursive call to BoundaryPM during inlining / Numeric
35	exception". The official runner avoids it via dfcc. To measure kill score
36	faithfully, mutate the source and call `_run_official.py ZopfliLZ77Optimal`
37	directly (~5s/run) — see /app/kill_zlz77opt_official.py. Hand-built dfcc with
38	`--dfcc __CPROVER__start` fails ("statement type 'input' not supported" / "name
39	not found in symbol_table") so reproducing the flow standalone isn't worth it;
40	just use the official runner.
41

file.content:
---
name: zlz77opt-depth200-vacuity
description: ZopfliLZ77Optimal verifies @200 with sound Fixed-sibling spec but 0/28 kills (depth-200 vacuity)
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: cf4817c1-02eb-4ae1-a917-45d46c8332fa
---

ZopfliLZ77Optimal (zopfli.c ~5247) — the squeeze driver, sibling of
[[zlof-depth200-vacuity]] (ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed). Verifies under the official
runner with a sound spec mirroring the Fixed sibling:
requires is_fresh(s)+is_fresh(store)+instart<=inend+ (inend-instart <
max_malloc_size/sizeof(float)-1); assigns(object_whole(s), object_whole(store)).
Unlike Fixed it never sets s->blockstart/blockend itself (GetBestLengths/
FollowPath don't either), so NO exact blockstart/blockend ensures available.

0/28 kills — inherent depth-200 vacuity, do NOT chase. Official flow is dfcc
enforce-contract with __CPROVER__start harness, all callee bodies INLINED (not
replaced), cbmc --depth 200. The whole call tree (greedy, LZ77OptimalRun→
GetBestLengths, ZopfliCalculateBlockSize→EncodeTree→BoundaryPM recursion) inlines
deep; every mutant's effect is past the depth-200 frontier:
- malloc-size mutants (blocksize-1, inend+instart): OOB only manifests deep
  inside inlined LZ77OptimalRun array writes; nothing caller-visible (arrays are
  local + freed). The zlof OBJECT_SIZE-callee-requires kill trick does NOT apply
  here because official flow INLINES (doesn't replace) LZ77OptimalRun.
- 7 verbose mutants (line `if (verbose_more || (verbose && cost<bestcost))`):
  EQUIVALENT — only affect an fprintf to stderr, no observable contract effect.
- loop-cond / cost<bestcost / i>5&&cost==lastcost / lastrandomstep mutants: all
  control which store/stats get produced, observable only after executing the
  huge inlined loop body, past depth 200.

Tooling notes: plain `goto-instrument --enforce-contract` (legacy, non-dfcc)
CRASHES here — "Recursive call to BoundaryPM during inlining / Numeric
exception". The official runner avoids it via dfcc. To measure kill score
faithfully, mutate the source and call `_run_official.py ZopfliLZ77Optimal`
directly (~5s/run) — see /app/kill_zlz77opt_official.py. Hand-built dfcc with
`--dfcc __CPROVER__start` fails ("statement type 'input' not supported" / "name
not found in symbol_table") so reproducing the flow standalone isn't worth it;
just use the official runner.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: zlof-depth200-vacuity
3	description: ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed verifies @depth200 but 0/3 kills (vacuity); spec proven strong (3/3 @depth800) via OBJECT_SIZE callee preconditions
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: dbdb1e48-314c-4b33-aa02-21489730bba0
8	---
9	
10	ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed (zopfli.c ~4199) verifies under the official harness
11	(`--depth 200`, PASS) but scores **0/3 @depth200** — a [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]]
12	case. All 3 mutants are on the prologue (length_array malloc `blocksize+1`→`-1`,
13	costs malloc `blocksize+1`→`-1`, `blocksize = inend-instart`→`inend+instart`).
14	
15	**The spec IS strong — proven 3/3 killable @depth800, just vacuous @200.** Lever:
16	the only place the allocation sizes are *checked* is the `LZ77OptimalRun(...)` call
17	site (callee replaced by contract), and that call sits past the depth-200 frontier
18	(prologue cost = 2 mallocs + ZopfliAllocHash's 7 is_fresh ensures + ZopfliCleanHash
19	frame). At depth 400 still vacuous; at depth 800 the `LZ77OptimalRun` precondition
20	assertions become reachable and flip: MUT-LA→precondition (length_array OBJECT_SIZE)
21	FAILURE, MUT-COSTS→costs OBJECT_SIZE FAILURE, MUT-BS→both FAILURE; ORIG all SUCCESS.
22	
23	**Key technique (reusable):** to kill a *caller's* malloc-size mutant when the
24	callee is contract-replaced, put the size requirement in the **callee's `requires`
25	as a plain boolean** `__CPROVER_OBJECT_SIZE(buf) == sizeof(T)*(n)`, NOT as
26	`__CPROVER_is_fresh(buf, sizeof(T)*n)`. Under this legacy `--replace-call-with-contract`
27	pipeline, **is_fresh in a callee requires is NOT asserted as a size check at the call
28	site** (under-alloc mutant survived even @depth800 with is_fresh); a plain OBJECT_SIZE
29	boolean predicate IS asserted and kills the mutants. Confirmed empirically.
30	
31	Edits made (both sound, both kept):
32	- LZ77OptimalRun: added `requires(OBJECT_SIZE(length_array)==sizeof(short)*(inend-instart+1))`
33	  and same for `costs` (genuine GetBestLengths requirement). LZ77OptimalRun stays
34	  UNVERIFIABLE — [[lor-fptr-param-crash]] rc134, unchanged by the added requires.
35	- ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed: `requires(is_fresh(s))`, `requires(instart<=inend)`,
36	  `requires(inend-instart < max_malloc_size/sizeof(float)-1)`, `assigns(s->blockstart,
37	  s->blockend)`, `ensures` exact-value on both. (Without these the deep proof fails on
38	  s-deref + unforwarded instart<=inend; @200 they're sound assumptions.)
39	
40	Other caller ZopfliLZ77Optimal has NO contract (rc6 @goto-instrument), pre-existing,
41	unaffected. Don't chase the @200 kills — pure vacuity. Scripts: /app/_verify_zlof.py
42	(official), /app/_score_zlof.py (3 mutants), /app/_verify_zlof.sh (manual, adjustable
43	depth), /app/_regress_check.py.
44

file.content:
---
name: zlof-depth200-vacuity
description: ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed verifies @depth200 but 0/3 kills (vacuity); spec proven strong (3/3 @depth800) via OBJECT_SIZE callee preconditions
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: dbdb1e48-314c-4b33-aa02-21489730bba0
---

ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed (zopfli.c ~4199) verifies under the official harness
(`--depth 200`, PASS) but scores **0/3 @depth200** — a [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]]
case. All 3 mutants are on the prologue (length_array malloc `blocksize+1`→`-1`,
costs malloc `blocksize+1`→`-1`, `blocksize = inend-instart`→`inend+instart`).

**The spec IS strong — proven 3/3 killable @depth800, just vacuous @200.** Lever:
the only place the allocation sizes are *checked* is the `LZ77OptimalRun(...)` call
site (callee replaced by contract), and that call sits past the depth-200 frontier
(prologue cost = 2 mallocs + ZopfliAllocHash's 7 is_fresh ensures + ZopfliCleanHash
frame). At depth 400 still vacuous; at depth 800 the `LZ77OptimalRun` precondition
assertions become reachable and flip: MUT-LA→precondition (length_array OBJECT_SIZE)
FAILURE, MUT-COSTS→costs OBJECT_SIZE FAILURE, MUT-BS→both FAILURE; ORIG all SUCCESS.

**Key technique (reusable):** to kill a *caller's* malloc-size mutant when the
callee is contract-replaced, put the size requirement in the **callee's `requires`
as a plain boolean** `__CPROVER_OBJECT_SIZE(buf) == sizeof(T)*(n)`, NOT as
`__CPROVER_is_fresh(buf, sizeof(T)*n)`. Under this legacy `--replace-call-with-contract`
pipeline, **is_fresh in a callee requires is NOT asserted as a size check at the call
site** (under-alloc mutant survived even @depth800 with is_fresh); a plain OBJECT_SIZE
boolean predicate IS asserted and kills the mutants. Confirmed empirically.

Edits made (both sound, both kept):
- LZ77OptimalRun: added `requires(OBJECT_SIZE(length_array)==sizeof(short)*(inend-instart+1))`
  and same for `costs` (genuine GetBestLengths requirement). LZ77OptimalRun stays
  UNVERIFIABLE — [[lor-fptr-param-crash]] rc134, unchanged by the added requires.
- ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed: `requires(is_fresh(s))`, `requires(instart<=inend)`,
  `requires(inend-instart < max_malloc_size/sizeof(float)-1)`, `assigns(s->blockstart,
  s->blockend)`, `ensures` exact-value on both. (Without these the deep proof fails on
  s-deref + unforwarded instart<=inend; @200 they're sound assumptions.)

Other caller ZopfliLZ77Optimal has NO contract (rc6 @goto-instrument), pre-existing,
unaffected. Don't chase the @200 kills — pure vacuity. Scripts: /app/_verify_zlof.py
(official), /app/_score_zlof.py (3 mutants), /app/_verify_zlof.sh (manual, adjustable
depth), /app/_regress_check.py.

content.content:
5225	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(stats))
5226	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
5227	    int k1; (0 <= k1 && k1 < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) ==> stats->litlens[k1] == 0 })
5228	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
5229	    int k2; (0 <= k2 && k2 < ZOPFLI_NUM_D) ==> stats->dists[k2] == 0 })
5230	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
5231	    int k3; (0 <= k3 && k3 < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) ==> stats->ll_symbols[k3] == 0 })
5232	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall {
5233	    int k4; (0 <= k4 && k4 < ZOPFLI_NUM_D) ==> stats->d_symbols[k4] == 0 })
5234	{
5235	    memset(stats->litlens, 0, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(stats->litlens[0]));
5236	    memset(stats->dists, 0, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(stats->dists[0]));
5237	
5238	    memset(stats->ll_symbols, 0, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(stats->ll_symbols[0]));
5239	    memset(stats->d_symbols, 0, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(stats->d_symbols[0]));
5240	}
5241	
5242	/*
5243	Calculates lit/len and dist pairs for given data.
5244	If instart is larger than 0, it uses values before instart as starting
5245	dictionary.
5246	*/
5247	void ZopfliLZ77Optimal(ZopfliBlockState *s,
5248	                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
5249	                       int numiterations,
5250	                       ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
5251	/* Squeeze driver, sibling of ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed.  The block state and the
5252	   output store are live, distinct objects; the parse walks [instart, inend) so
5253	   instart never exceeds inend.  The block-length bound keeps the two
5254	   `malloc(... * (blocksize + 1))` sizings (for length_array and costs) inside
5255	   CBMC's max object size, mirroring the Fixed sibling's bound.  Unlike the Fixed
5256	   variant this function never assigns s->blockstart / s->blockend itself, so no
5257	   exact-value postcondition on them is available; the frame allows the whole
5258	   state and store objects to change, since the output is produced deep inside
5259	   the inlined LZ77OptimalRun / ZopfliCopyLZ77Store calls. */

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	# Kill-score for ZopfliLZ77Optimal using the EXACT official runner (run_cbmc)
3	# applied to the mutated source.  Mutate -> run official -> PASS=survived /
4	# (FAIL or rc!=0)=killed -> restore.
5	import re, subprocess, sys, shutil
6	
7	SRC = "/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c"
8	FUNCTION = "ZopfliLZ77Optimal"
9	BACKUP = "/tmp/zopfli_orig_zlz77opt.c"
10	
11	shutil.copy(SRC, BACKUP)
12	mut_text = subprocess.run(
13	    ["get-mutants", "--function", FUNCTION, "--file", SRC],
14	    capture_output=True, text=True).stdout
15	
16	blocks = re.split(r'(?m)^--- original$', mut_text)
17	muts = []
18	for b in blocks:
19	    m = re.search(r'@@ -(\d+) \+\d+ @@', b)
20	    if not m:
21	        continue
22	    line = int(m.group(1))
23	    plus = [l for l in b.splitlines() if l.startswith('+') and not l.startswith('+++')]
24	    minus = [l for l in b.splitlines() if l.startswith('-') and not l.startswith('---')]
25	    if not plus or not minus:
26	        continue
27	    muts.append((line, minus[0][1:], plus[0][1:]))
28	
29	orig_lines = open(BACKUP).read().split('\n')
30	killed = 0
31	survived = []
32	try:
33	    for idx, (line, orig, mut) in enumerate(muts):
34	        if orig_lines[line-1] != orig:
35	            print(f"LINEMISS  {idx} L{line}: got {orig_lines[line-1]!r} want {orig!r}")
36	            continue
37	        new = list(orig_lines)
38	        new[line-1] = mut
39	        open(SRC, "w").write('\n'.join(new))
40	        r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, "/app/_run_official.py", FUNCTION],
41	                           capture_output=True, text=True, cwd="/app")
42	        out = r.stdout + r.stderr
43	        if "-> PASS" in out:
44	            survived.append((idx, line, mut.strip()))
45	            print(f"SURVIVED  {idx} L{line}: {mut.strip()}")
46	        else:
47	            killed += 1
48	            print(f"KILLED    {idx} L{line}: {mut.strip()}")
49	finally:
50	    shutil.copy(BACKUP, SRC)
51	
52	print(f"\n=== Killed {killed} / {len(muts)} ===")
53	if survived:
54	    print("Survivors:")
55	    for idx, line, m in survived:
56	        print(f"  {idx} L{line}: {m}")
57

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Kill-score for ZopfliLZ77Optimal using the EXACT official runner (run_cbmc)
# applied to the mutated source.  Mutate -> run official -> PASS=survived /
# (FAIL or rc!=0)=killed -> restore.
import re, subprocess, sys, shutil

SRC = "/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c"
FUNCTION = "ZopfliLZ77Optimal"
BACKUP = "/tmp/zopfli_orig_zlz77opt.c"

shutil.copy(SRC, BACKUP)
mut_text = subprocess.run(
    ["get-mutants", "--function", FUNCTION, "--file", SRC],
    capture_output=True, text=True).stdout

blocks = re.split(r'(?m)^--- original$', mut_text)
muts = []
for b in blocks:
    m = re.search(r'@@ -(\d+) \+\d+ @@', b)
    if not m:
        continue
    line = int(m.group(1))
    plus = [l for l in b.splitlines() if l.startswith('+') and not l.startswith('+++')]
    minus = [l for l in b.splitlines() if l.startswith('-') and not l.startswith('---')]
    if not plus or not minus:
        continue
    muts.append((line, minus[0][1:], plus[0][1:]))

orig_lines = open(BACKUP).read().split('\n')
killed = 0
survived = []
try:
    for idx, (line, orig, mut) in enumerate(muts):
        if orig_lines[line-1] != orig:
            print(f"LINEMISS  {idx} L{line}: got {orig_lines[line-1]!r} want {orig!r}")
            continue
        new = list(orig_lines)
        new[line-1] = mut
        open(SRC, "w").write('\n'.join(new))
        r = subprocess.run([sys.executable, "/app/_run_official.py", FUNCTION],
                           capture_output=True, text=True, cwd="/app")
        out = r.stdout + r.stderr
        if "-> PASS" in out:
            survived.append((idx, line, mut.strip()))
            print(f"SURVIVED  {idx} L{line}: {mut.strip()}")
        else:
            killed += 1
            print(f"KILLED    {idx} L{line}: {mut.strip()}")
finally:
    shutil.copy(BACKUP, SRC)

print(f"\n=== Killed {killed} / {len(muts)} ===")
if survived:
    print("Survivors:")
    for idx, line, m in survived:
        print(f"  {idx} L{line}: {m}")

ZopfliLZ77Optimal **already verifies** (`PASS`, rc=0) under the official runner with the sound spec already in place, and I've confirmed the file matches my prior investigation exactly.

## Summary

**Status: verified, spec already maximal — kills not chaseable.**

The function carries a sound contract (lines 5260–5264):
- `requires is_fresh(s)`, `is_fresh(store)`, `instart <= inend`, and `inend - instart < max_malloc_size/sizeof(float) - 1` (bounds the two `malloc(... * (blocksize+1))` sizings)
- `assigns object_whole(s), object_whole(store)`

Unlike its Fixed sibling, this function never sets `s->blockstart`/`s->blockend` itself (neither do `GetBestLengths`/`FollowPath`), so no exact-value postcondition is available.

**The 0/28 kill score is inherent depth-200 vacuity, not a weak spec** — I established this empirically in a prior session and the situation is unchanged:

- **malloc-size mutants** (`blocksize-1`, `inend+instart`): OOB only manifests deep inside the *inlined* `LZ77OptimalRun` array writes — nothing caller-visible (arrays are local + freed). The `zlof` OBJECT_SIZE-callee-requires trick does **not** apply because the official flow **inlines** `LZ77OptimalRun` rather than contract-replacing it.
- **7 verbose mutants** (`if (verbose_more || (verbose && cost<bestcost))`): genuinely **equivalent** — they only affect an `fprintf` to stderr, no observable contract effect.
- **loop-cond / `cost<bestcost` / `i>5&&cost==lastcost` / `lastrandomstep` mutants**: all control which store/stats are produced, observable only after executing the huge inlined loop body — past the depth-200 frontier.

This is one of the documented [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]] cases. Per the project rule against chasing inherently-unkillable mutants (and my own memory `[[zlz77opt-depth200-vacuity]]`), no further edits are warranted — the spec is already as strong as the depth-200 official flow allows. No changes made.
